seoTitle: Create a Survey From Scratch in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Build a responsive online survey in Microsoft Forms with multiple-choice, rating, and text questions, then apply a theme and share the link.
intro: Assembling a survey that renders cleanly on any device and collects usable data takes more than dropping in random questions. This guide covers building a Microsoft Forms survey from a blank canvas, structuring your question types, and getting a shareable link ready to send.

steps.heading: Create a new form
steps.body: Click New Form and enter a clear, descriptive title for your survey.

steps.heading: Add your question types
steps.body: Use Add New to combine multiple-choice selections, rating indicators, and long-form text rows depending on what you need to measure.

steps.heading: Mark required fields
steps.body: Toggle Required on any question where skipping would leave gaps in your data.

steps.heading: Apply a visual theme
steps.body: Open the Style panel and choose an accent color or background that fits your brand or audience.

steps.heading: Collect and share your link
steps.body: Click Collect Responses to grab a shortened, shareable URL for distributing the survey.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to build a basic survey?
faq.answer: Combine a few Choice questions for quick data with one Long Answer text field for open feedback, then apply a theme and share.

faq.question: Can I preview how the survey looks on mobile before sending it?
faq.answer: Yes, the Preview button lets you test both desktop and mobile layouts before publishing.

faq.question: Do I need a Microsoft 365 account to create a survey?
faq.answer: Yes, Microsoft Forms requires signing in through the Microsoft 365 portal to build and manage surveys.

faq.question: How do respondents access my finished survey?
faq.answer: Use the Collect Responses button to generate a shortened link you can share by email, chat, or social channels.
